biases_detected: shell_tool_preference_violations:RECURRING:worsening — units f22bd641-0, f22bd641-12, 8d9169bc-0, 2527f606-1, 2527f606-10, 2527f606-16 all use Bash with /usr/bin/grep or find|xargs grep instead of Grep/Glob tools; unit 2527f606-10 shows extreme thrash: rg failed, python3 inline fallback, then /usr/bin/grep — three approaches before getting output that Grep tool would have produced in one call; frequency not decreasing vs prior cycles

re_edit_thrash:RECURRING:worsening — units 2527f606-14 (5+ Edit+Read cycles on job-output-cleanup.md), 59c741e5-37 (7 consecutive Read calls on index.ts before editing), 59c741e5-45 (10+ Read+Edit cycles on index.ts), 59c741e5-50 (multiple Read passes on same two files); pattern is more frequent and higher cycle-count than prior sessions

api_discovery_thrash:RECURRING:stable — unit 59c741e5-45 attempts gum table with two different invocation styles (pipe with headers, printf with tab-sep) both failing before running gum --help; structurally identical to prior fiber-snatcher probe-then-fail pattern; docs-first never applied

intra_session_tsc_path_repeat:NEW — unit 59c741e5-21 tries node_modules/.bin/tsc twice (both fail), then finds bun works; unit 59c741e5-37 in the same session tries node_modules/.bin/tsc twice again before switching to bun; unit 59c741e5-50 tries bun tsc then node_modules/.bin/tsc again; no within-session caching of 'bun works, tsc does not' — distinct from cross-session environment_state_assumption because the correction happened in-session and was not retained across units
environment_state_assumption:RECURRING:stable — tsc path assumption repeated across units 59c741e5-21, 37, 50; cp without flags in 59c741e5-1 required yes|cp -f workaround on third attempt

mcp_fallback_without_retry:INSTANCE — unit f22bd641-2 calls mcp__file-tools__list_sheets once, it fails (success=false), then immediately pivots to node/ExcelJS Bash scripts without a retry or error-inspection; matches dream insight 5 (external integrations are weakest links); not a new bias class but first appearance in this dataset

cross_references.note: CONFIRMED — input length 34, zero tools, 172-char response, accepted; agent correctly interpreted terse acknowledgment as continuation signal without asking clarifying questions
CONFIRMED — mcp__file-tools__list_sheets failed immediately (first tool attempted) and agent pivoted to Bash/node fallback; the weakness manifested exactly at the MCP boundary as predicted

PARTIAL VIOLATION — agent successfully restored checkpoints from Trash but had to use yes|cp -f workaround; state recovery worked but the mechanism was fragile; consistent with insight that persistence abstractions have boundary-crossing failure modes

VIOLATED — gum output was attempted but gum table failed; agent did not proactively take a screenshot or render output to verify before presenting to user; dry-run verification step was present but not applied to UI rendering

recommendations: Highest priority: add within-session command-result cache — when a tool invocation pattern succeeds, record it; when the same pattern (tsc path, grep flags) previously failed in the same session, skip it rather than re-anchoring; this targets intra_session_tsc_path_repeat which is new and not addressed by any prior recommendation

For api_discovery_thrash: enforce docs-first rule as a hard precondition before any trial invocation of external binaries (gum, fiber-snatcher, etc.); pattern has not improved across multiple cycles; a pre-tool-call check of --help output would eliminate most thrash

Deprioritize re_edit_thrash and shell_tool_preference_violations recommendations — both have been flagged in every prior cycle with no improvement trajectory; escalation to a hook or hard stop after N consecutive same-file edits may be required rather than another text recommendation

For mcp_fallback_without_retry: when an MCP tool call returns success=false on first attempt for a file-read operation, log the error before pivoting — the failure reason (format unsupported vs network vs path issue) should determine whether to retry with different params or fall back to Bash
